postData.contentHtml:
New Zealand has firmly established its dominance over England in the second test match at The Oval, building a formidable lead of over 400 runs by lunch on day four. This crucial performance has significantly bolstered their chances of leveling the series.

The Black Caps’ relentless batting display saw them reach a staggering 445-run lead by the mid-day break, putting immense pressure on the English side. Despite England's Jofra Archer managing to secure two wickets during this period, New Zealand's batsmen held strong, meticulously adding to their advantage.

The test match has generated considerable buzz among cricket enthusiasts. Social media sentiment surrounding the New Zealand-England cricket series is largely positive, reflecting the excitement and engaging nature of the contest, though some debates and discussions are naturally unfolding among fans as the match progresses.
postData.title: New Zealand Dominates England, Extends Lead to Over 400 on Day Four
postData.summary: New Zealand has taken a commanding lead against England in their second test match at The Oval, pushing their advantage beyond 400 runs by lunch on day four. This strong performance puts them in a prime position to level the series.

sources.title: Cricket-New Zealand stretch lead to over 400 against England on day four
New Zealand stretch lead to over 400 against England on day four
